---
title: "Fullington_diversity_analysis"
author: "Smriti"
date: "2025-06-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
#Install packages
```{r}
pacman::p_load(tidyverse, dplyr, readr, codyn, lme4, lmerTest, ggrepel, tictoc, ggpubr, nlme)

```
#data upload
```{r}
df_all <- read.csv("df_all.csv")
```
#Community structure (richness, evenness, dominance)
```{r}
colnames(df_all)
length(unique(df_all$Quadrat))

#Averaging by cluster, quadrat, and species
df_all.avg.rac <- df_all %>%  dplyr::group_by(Clusters_complete_silhouette, Quadrat, Species) %>% dplyr::summarize(stem_count=n()) %>%  mutate(rank = rank(desc(stem_count))) %>% filter(Species!="Corner")


# Identify quadrats with multiple clusters
quadrat_with_one_cluster <- df_all.avg.rac %>%
  group_by(Quadrat) %>%                     # Group by quadrat
  summarise(n_clusters = n_distinct(Clusters_complete_silhouette)) %>% # Count unique land-use types
  filter(n_clusters == 1) 

# Filter the original dataframe to keep only these quadrats
df_one_cluster <- df_all.avg.rac %>%
  filter(Quadrat %in% quadrat_with_one_cluster$Quadrat)


#Measuring rank abundance curve difference for the two land use types
colnames(df_one_cluster)
colnames(df_all)
df_one_cluster$stem_count=as.numeric(df_one_cluster$stem_count)
df_one_cluster$Quadrat=as.character(df_one_cluster$Quadrat)


###community structure
Richness_evenness <- community_structure(
df_one_cluster,
time.var = NULL,
abundance.var = "stem_count",
replicate.var = "Quadrat",
metric = c("Evar", "SimpsonEvenness", "EQ")
)

#add land_use info
df_quadrat_info <- df_one_cluster %>% dplyr::select(Quadrat, Clusters_complete_silhouette)
Richness_evenness_cluster <- merge(Richness_evenness, df_quadrat_info, by="Quadrat", all.x=FALSE) %>% unique() %>%  drop_na(Evar, Clusters_complete_silhouette, Quadrat)

# Model for richness
model_richness <- lme(richness ~ Clusters_complete_silhouette,
                      random = ~1 | Quadrat,
                      data = Richness_evenness_cluster)
summary(model_richness)
anova(model_richness)
##Kruskal-Wallis richness
kruskal.test(richness ~ Clusters_complete_silhouette, data = Richness_evenness_cluster)

pairwise.wilcox.test(Richness_evenness_cluster$richness, Richness_evenness_cluster$Clusters_complete_silhouette, p.adjust.method = "BH")

# Convert cluster to factor with informative labels
Richness_evenness_cluster$Cluster <- factor(Richness_evenness_cluster$Clusters_complete_silhouette,
                                   levels = c(1, 2, 3),
                                   labels = c("Cluster 1", "Cluster 2", "Cluster 3"))

# Define pairwise comparisons
my_comparisons <- list(c("1", "2"), c("1", "3"), c("2", "3"))

# Plot
richness_cluster_fig <- ggplot(Richness_evenness_cluster, aes(x = as.factor(Clusters_complete_silhouette), y = richness)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "slateblue", outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.4, size = 2,alpha = 0.7, color= "black")  +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif", size = 6, bracket.size = 1.2)  + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 22))+
  labs(
    x = "Cluster",
    y = "Species Richness"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1.2),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )




# Model for evenness
model_evenness <- lme(Evar ~ Clusters_complete_silhouette,
                      random = ~1 | Quadrat,
                      data = Richness_evenness_cluster)

summary(model_evenness)
anova(model_evenness)

##Kruskal-Wallis evenness
kruskal.test(Evar ~ Clusters_complete_silhouette, data = Richness_evenness_cluster)

pairwise.wilcox.test(Richness_evenness_cluster$Evar, Richness_evenness_cluster$Clusters_complete_silhouette, p.adjust.method = "BH")

# Convert cluster to factor with informative labels
Richness_evenness_cluster$Cluster <- factor(Richness_evenness_cluster$Clusters_complete_silhouette,
                                   levels = c(1, 2, 3),
                                   labels = c("Cluster 1", "Cluster 2", "Cluster 3"))

# Define pairwise comparisons
my_comparisons <- list(c("1", "2"), c("1", "3"), c("2", "3"))

# Plot
evenness_cluster_fig <- ggplot(Richness_evenness_cluster, aes(x = as.factor(Clusters_complete_silhouette), y = Evar)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "slateblue", outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, size = 2, color = "black")  +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif", size = 6, bracket.size = 1.2) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Cluster",
    y = "Species Evenness"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1.2),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )


## Dominance

# First, calculate relative abundances
dominance_df <- df_one_cluster %>%
  group_by(Quadrat, Species) %>%
  summarize(abundance = sum(stem_count), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(Quadrat) %>%
  mutate(p = abundance / sum(abundance)) %>%
  summarize(Dominance = sum(p^2), .groups = "drop")

#remove species column from df_one_cluster
colnames(df_one_cluster)
df_one_cluster_no_species <- df_one_cluster %>% dplyr::select(-c(Species, stem_count, rank))

dominance_merged <- dominance_df %>%
  left_join(df_one_cluster_no_species, by = "Quadrat") %>% unique()

##dominance model
model_dominance <- lme(Dominance ~ Clusters_complete_silhouette,
                      random = ~1 | Quadrat,
                      data = dominance_merged)

summary(model_dominance)
anova(model_dominance)

##Kruskal-Wallis dominance
kruskal.test(Dominance ~ Clusters_complete_silhouette, data = dominance_merged)

pairwise.wilcox.test(dominance_merged$Dominance, dominance_merged$Clusters_complete_silhouette, p.adjust.method = "BH")

# Convert cluster to factor with informative labels
dominance_merged$Cluster <- factor(dominance_merged$Clusters_complete_silhouette,
                                   levels = c(1, 2, 3),
                                   labels = c("Cluster 1", "Cluster 2", "Cluster 3"))

# Define pairwise comparisons
my_comparisons <- list(c("1", "2"), c("1", "3"), c("2", "3"))

# Plot
dominance_cluster_fig <- ggplot(dominance_merged, aes(x = as.factor(Clusters_complete_silhouette), y = Dominance)) +
  geom_boxplot(fill = "slateblue", outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.4, alpha = 0.7, size = 2, color= "black")  +
  stat_compare_means(comparisons = my_comparisons, method = "wilcox.test", label = "p.signif", size = 6, bracket.size = 1.2) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Cluster",
    y = "Simpson's Dominance Index"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 20) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 1.2),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

##combined figure
combined_diversity_fig <- ggarrange(
  richness_cluster_fig, evenness_cluster_fig,
  labels = c("A)", "B)",
  ncol = 2, nrow = 1,
  align = "v",
  heights = c(1, 1) 
))

ggsave("combined.diversity.png", combined_diversity_fig, dpi = 300, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in")

```
